explanation: This statement expresses a conditional: whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold at the same time. In LTL, this is globally (for all times), if prop_0 is true then prop_1 is also true.

The sentence states that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 should also hold at that time. This is a safety property, and it can be expressed using the 'globally' (G) operator with implication: globally, if prop_0 then prop_1.
This statement means that whenever prop_0 holds, then at the same time prop_1 must also hold. This expresses an invariant: globally, prop_0 implies prop_1.
This statement expresses that whenever 'prop_0' holds, 'prop_1' must also hold at the same time. This can be represented in LTL as 'globally, (if prop_0 then prop_1)'.

The phrase 'When prop_0, the prop_1.' suggests that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 should also hold at the same time. This is typically represented in LTL as 'globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 holds'.
The sentence expresses that whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold at that moment. In LTL, this is captured as: globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 is also true.
The meaning is: whenever prop_0 holds, then at the same time, prop_1 must hold. This is typically encoded as 'globally, if prop_0, then prop_1'.
This prompt indicates that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must hold at that same time. In LTL, this property is expressed as 'globally, if prop_0 then prop_1.'
This statement means that whenever 'prop_0' holds, then 'prop_1' must also hold at the same time. This is typically captured as 'globally, if prop_0 then prop_1'.
This statement means that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at the same time. In LTL, this is usually represented globally: always if prop_0, then prop_1.
